summary: Static keyword matches in bundled Vite-like build tool; behavioral corroboration missing
details: Package name (@vite-ln/build-ts) suggests a Vite-family build/TypeScript tool. Bundled files in dist/node/ (dep-Cy9twKMn.js, dep-SDtFYyy1.js, runtime.js) trigger keyword-cooccurrence patterns: an "install" string plus multiple `curl` substrings in a large minified chunk, and `Buffer.from(..., "base64").toString(...)` decodings. These patterns are consistent with normal contents of a bundled build toolchain: Vite/Rollup bundles routinely embed lockfile/npm-help text (containing "install" and `curl` snippets from documentation), and base64 decoding of source maps, inlined assets, and encoded constants is standard in build tooling. No specific installer-side data read, no hardcoded attacker C2 endpoint, no lifecycle hook exec of fetched content, and no credential-path access have been demonstrated. The @vite-ln/* scope is not an established Vite publisher, so name-adjacency to Vite is a review-worthy signal on its own but does not by itself constitute an attack fingerprint. Reviewer should confirm whether this is a legitimate Vite fork/derivative or a lookalike, and whether any of the flagged base64/curl occurrences are wired to an install-time or import-time execution path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
